mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses consumer spending expectations and corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals synchronized consumer and business demand recovery, which increases equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to generate positive 60-day forward returns in at least 60 percent of observed instances, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly contractions in industrial production indicate immediate supply-side bottlenecks and reduced economic activity, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth fails to predict negative 5-day forward returns, with a win rate below 45 percent during identified liquidity-constrained periods, or Rank IC remains negative.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of near-zero federal funds rates and rising unemployment creates a policy divergence where monetary stimulus fails to stimulate real activity, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint condition of low rates and high unemployment yields positive 20-day returns in over 50 percent of cases, or the interaction effect shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 0.5 percent signals severe demand weakness that forces corporate earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below 0.5 percent, or the threshold model fails out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic momentum and higher corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS revisions fail to predict positive 5-day forward returns, with a directional accuracy below 50 percent across a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels alongside stable federal funds rates indicate emerging inflationary pressures that erode real corporate margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Elevated CPIAUCSL growth fails to predict negative 20-day returns when FEDFUNDS remains stable, with a win rate below 40 percent, or the mechanism shows no statistical significance in regime-filtered backtests.
